accurate.why: All claims are supported by the bill text or accurate context (Sen. Andy Kim, Foreign Relations referral, War Powers framework).

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the sponsor, date, committee, and operative provisions all match the bill and verified records.

states_purpose.why: The summary clearly states the bill directs removal of U.S. Armed Forces from unauthorized hostilities within or against Iran.

changes_to_existing_law.why: The summary correctly invokes the War Powers Resolution and the expedited-procedure statutes (Section 1013 and Section 601(b)) the bill relies on.

exceptions_conditions.why: The summary captures the rule-of-construction carve-outs (self-defense, intelligence sharing, defensive assistance to Israel, evacuation) and the declaration/authorization exception.

effective_dates.why: The summary correctly notes the introduction date and that no separate effective date is specified.

major_provisions_covered.why: The summary captures the operative directive, findings, expedited procedures, and rule of construction—the bill's essential effects.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral, attributing characterizations (e.g. 'war') to the findings without editorializing.

coherent.why: The summary is well-organized and internally consistent.

concise.why: The summary is appropriately compact with no padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: Sen. Kim, the Committee on Foreign Relations, and the named statutes are all correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: The figures (13 killed, at least 350 wounded, April 10/13, 2026 dates) match the bill text.
